root_text.an5.48:1.1: “Pañcimāni, bhikkhave, alabbhanīyāni ṭhānāni samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.48:1.3: ‘Jarādhammaṁ mā jīrī’ti alabbhanīyaṁ ṭhānaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.48:1.4: ‘Byādhidhammaṁ mā byādhīyī’ti …pe… ‘maraṇadhammaṁ mā mīyī’ti … ‘khayadhammaṁ mā khīyī’ti … ‘nassanadhammaṁ mā nassī’ti alabbhanīyaṁ ṭhānaṁ samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasmiṁ.
root_text.an5.48:2.2: So jarādhamme jiṇṇe na iti paṭisañcikkhati:
root_text.an5.48:2.3: ‘na kho mayhevekassa jarādhammaṁ jīrati, atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbesaṁ sattānaṁ jarādhammaṁ jīrati.
root_text.an5.48:2.4: Ahañceva kho pana jarādhamme jiṇṇe soceyyaṁ kilameyyaṁ parideveyyaṁ, urattāḷiṁ kandeyyaṁ, sammohaṁ āpajjeyyaṁ, bhattampi me nacchādeyya, kāyepi dubbaṇṇiyaṁ okkameyya, kammantāpi nappavatteyyuṁ, amittāpi attamanā assu, mittāpi dummanā assū’ti.
root_text.an5.48:2.5: So jarādhamme jiṇṇe socati kilamati paridevati, urattāḷiṁ kandati, sammohaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an5.48:2.7: ‘assutavā puthujjano viddho savisena sokasallena attānaṁyeva paritāpeti’.
root_text.an5.48:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, assutavato puthujjanassa byādhidhammaṁ byādhīyati …pe… maraṇadhammaṁ mīyati … khayadhammaṁ khīyati … nassanadhammaṁ nassati.
root_text.an5.48:3.2: So nassanadhamme naṭṭhe na iti paṭisañcikkhati:
root_text.an5.48:3.3: ‘na kho mayhevekassa nassanadhammaṁ nassati, atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbesaṁ sattānaṁ nassanadhammaṁ nassati.
root_text.an5.48:3.4: Ahañceva kho pana nassanadhamme naṭṭhe soceyyaṁ kilameyyaṁ parideveyyaṁ, urattāḷiṁ kandeyyaṁ, sammohaṁ āpajjeyyaṁ, bhattampi me nacchādeyya, kāyepi dubbaṇṇiyaṁ okkameyya, kammantāpi nappavatteyyuṁ, amittāpi attamanā assu, mittāpi dummanā assū’ti.
root_text.an5.48:3.5: So nassanadhamme naṭṭhe socati kilamati paridevati, urattāḷiṁ kandati, sammohaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an5.48:3.7: ‘assutavā puthujjano viddho savisena sokasallena attānaṁyeva paritāpeti’.
root_text.an5.48:4.1: Sutavato ca kho, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa jarādhammaṁ jīrati.
root_text.an5.48:4.3: ‘na kho mayhevekassa jarādhammaṁ jīrati, atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbesaṁ sattānaṁ jarādhammaṁ jīrati.
root_text.an5.48:4.4: Ahañceva kho pana jarādhamme jiṇṇe soceyyaṁ kilameyyaṁ parideveyyaṁ, urattāḷiṁ kandeyyaṁ, sammohaṁ āpajjeyyaṁ, bhattampi me nacchādeyya, kāyepi dubbaṇṇiyaṁ okkameyya, kammantāpi nappavatteyyuṁ, amittāpi attamanā assu, mittāpi dummanā assū’ti.
root_text.an5.48:4.5: So jarādhamme jiṇṇe na socati na kilamati na paridevati, na urattāḷiṁ kandati, na sammohaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an5.48:4.7: ‘sutavā ariyasāvako abbuhi savisaṁ sokasallaṁ, yena viddho assutavā puthujjano attānaṁyeva paritāpeti.
root_text.an5.48:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, sutavato ariyasāvakassa byādhidhammaṁ byādhīyati …pe… maraṇadhammaṁ mīyati … khayadhammaṁ khīyati … nassanadhammaṁ nassati.
root_text.an5.48:5.3: ‘na kho mayhevekassa nassanadhammaṁ nassati, atha kho yāvatā sattānaṁ āgati gati cuti upapatti sabbesaṁ sattānaṁ nassanadhammaṁ nassati.
root_text.an5.48:5.4: Ahañceva kho pana nassanadhamme naṭṭhe soceyyaṁ kilameyyaṁ parideveyyaṁ, urattāḷiṁ kandeyyaṁ, sammohaṁ āpajjeyyaṁ, bhattampi me nacchādeyya, kāyepi dubbaṇṇiyaṁ okkameyya, kammantāpi nappavatteyyuṁ, amittāpi attamanā assu, mittāpi dummanā assū’ti.
root_text.an5.48:5.5: So nassanadhamme naṭṭhe na socati na kilamati na paridevati, na urattāḷiṁ kandati, na sammohaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an5.48:5.7: ‘sutavā ariyasāvako abbuhi savisaṁ sokasallaṁ, yena viddho assutavā puthujjano attānaṁyeva paritāpeti.
root_text.an5.48:6.1: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, pañca alabbhanīyāni ṭhānāni samaṇena vā brāhmaṇena vā devena vā mārena vā brahmunā vā kenaci vā lokasminti.

translation_text.an5.48:1.1: “Mendicants, there are five things that cannot be had by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or divinity or by anyone in the world.
translation_text.an5.48:1.3: That someone liable to old age should not grow old.
translation_text.an5.48:1.4: That someone liable to sickness should not get sick. … That someone liable to death should not die. … That someone liable to ending should not end. … That someone liable to perishing should not perish. …
translation_text.an5.48:2.1: An unlearned ordinary person has someone liable to old age who grows old.
translation_text.an5.48:2.2: But they don’t reflect on old age:
translation_text.an5.48:2.3: ‘It’s not just me who has someone liable to old age who grows old. For as long as sentient beings come and go, pass away and are reborn, they all have someone liable to old age who grows old.
translation_text.an5.48:2.4: If I were to sorrow and wail and lament, beating my breast and falling into confusion, just because someone liable to old age grows old, I’d lose my appetite and my physical appearance would deteriorate. My work wouldn’t get done, my enemies would be encouraged, and my friends would be dispirited.’
translation_text.an5.48:2.5: And so, when someone liable to old age grows old, they sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion.
translation_text.an5.48:2.7: an unlearned ordinary person struck by sorrow’s poisoned arrow, who only mortifies themselves.
translation_text.an5.48:3.1: Furthermore, an unlearned ordinary person has someone liable to sickness … death … ending … perishing.
translation_text.an5.48:3.2: But they don’t reflect on perishing:
translation_text.an5.48:3.3: ‘It’s not just me who has someone liable to perishing who perishes. For as long as sentient beings come and go, pass away and are reborn, they all have someone liable to perishing who perishes.
translation_text.an5.48:3.4: If I were to sorrow and wail and lament, beating my breast and falling into confusion, just because someone liable to perishing perishes, I’d lose my appetite and my physical appearance would deteriorate. My work wouldn’t get done, my enemies would be encouraged, and my friends would be dispirited.’
translation_text.an5.48:3.5: And so, when someone liable to perishing perishes, they sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion.
translation_text.an5.48:3.7: an unlearned ordinary person struck by sorrow’s poisoned arrow, who only mortifies themselves.
translation_text.an5.48:4.1: A learned noble disciple has someone liable to old age who grows old.
translation_text.an5.48:4.2: So they reflect on old age:
translation_text.an5.48:4.3: ‘It’s not just me who has someone liable to old age who grows old. For as long as sentient beings come and go, pass away and are reborn, they all have someone liable to old age who grows old.
translation_text.an5.48:4.4: If I were to sorrow and wail and lament, beating my breast and falling into confusion, just because someone liable to old age grows old, I’d lose my appetite and my physical appearance would deteriorate. My work wouldn’t get done, my enemies would be encouraged, and my friends would be dispirited.’
translation_text.an5.48:4.5: And so, when someone liable to old age grows old, they don’t sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion.
translation_text.an5.48:4.7: a learned noble disciple who has drawn out sorrow’s poisoned arrow, struck by which unlearned ordinary people only mortify themselves.
translation_text.an5.48:4.8: Sorrowless, free of thorns, that noble disciple only extinguishes themselves.
translation_text.an5.48:5.1: Furthermore, a learned noble disciple has someone liable to sickness … death … ending … perishing.
translation_text.an5.48:5.3: ‘It’s not just me who has someone liable to perishing who perishes. For as long as sentient beings come and go, pass away and are reborn, they all have someone liable to perishing who perishes.
translation_text.an5.48:5.4: If I were to sorrow and wail and lament, beating my breast and falling into confusion, just because someone liable to perishing perishes, I’d lose my appetite and my physical appearance would deteriorate. My work wouldn’t get done, my enemies would be encouraged, and my friends would be dispirited.’
translation_text.an5.48:5.5: And so, when someone liable to perishing perishes, they don’t sorrow and wail and lament, beating their breast and falling into confusion.
translation_text.an5.48:5.7: a learned noble disciple who has drawn out sorrow’s poisoned arrow, struck by which unlearned ordinary people only mortify themselves.
translation_text.an5.48:5.8: Sorrowless, free of thorns, that noble disciple only extinguishes themselves.
translation_text.an5.48:6.1: These are the five things that cannot be had by any ascetic or brahmin or god or Māra or divinity or by anyone in the world.
translation_text.an5.48:7.2: doesn’t do even a little bit of good.
translation_text.an5.48:7.3: When they know that you’re sad,
translation_text.an5.48:8.1: When an astute person doesn’t waver <j>in the face of adversity,
translation_text.an5.48:8.2: as they’re able to evaluate what’s beneficial,
translation_text.an5.48:8.4: seeing that their normal expression doesn’t change.
translation_text.an5.48:9.3: if by means of any such things you benefit,
translation_text.an5.48:9.4: then by all means keep doing them.
translation_text.an5.48:10.1: But if you understand that ‘this good thing
translation_text.an5.48:10.2: can’t be had by me or by anyone else’,
translation_text.an5.48:10.3: you should accept it without sorrowing, thinking:
translation_text.an5.48:10.4: ‘The deed is powerful. What can I do now?’”

variant_text.an5.48:2.4: Ahañceva → ahañce (?) | kammantāpi nappavatteyyuṁ → kammantopi nappavatteyya (mr)
variant_text.an5.48:9.3: Yathā yathā yattha → yathā yathā yattha yattha (mr)
